suggested_topics: the scariest bug you ever shipped to production
why your houseplants are harder to keep alive than you think
the actual physics of why cats always land on their feet
why do we yawn and why is it contagious
the closest star to Earth and what we actually know about it
extra_system_rules: Sound like a real person on Reddit or Twitter, NOT like a philosophy textbook.
NO academic jargon: no 'credence,' 'posterior probability,' 'empirical,' 'warrants scrutiny.'
Write about REAL WORLD topics: food, cities, sports, technology, nature, history — not abstract concepts.

formats.name: three_word_saga
formats.instruction: Convey an entire story or idea using just THREE WORDS. Make them evocative, unexpected, and memorable. No punctuation.

formats.name: imaginary_review
formats.instruction: Write a comedic review of a thing or idea that doesn't exist. Rate it out of 5 stars, describe its imaginary strengths and flaws in 1-2 sentences.

formats.name: rapid_fire_rant
formats.instruction: Write a passionate rant about a minor annoyance. Deliver your point in quick bursts: 5 short lines, each under 12 words. No explanations.

formats.name: microfiction
formats.instruction: Invent a fictional scenario in a single paragraph. Set up, twist, and punchline all in under 50 words.

formats.name: time_traveler_note
formats.instruction: Write a note from the perspective of a time traveler visiting the present. Share observations, warnings, or advice in 100-250 words.

formats.name: reverse_how_to
formats.instruction: Describe how NOT to do something, using exaggerated steps and warnings. Be playful, but informative. 80-200 words.

formats.name: easter_egg_expose
formats.instruction: Reveal a hidden feature or surprising detail about a familiar topic. Give context, share the discovery moment, and explain its impact in 120-300 words.

formats.name: contrarian_manifesto
formats.instruction: Write a mini-manifesto outlining a mainstream belief you reject. Argue boldly in 3 sections: why the belief exists, why you disagree, what you propose instead. 200-400 words.

title_styles: Write a title as an urgent message in ALL CAPS, as if you're warning the world.
Write a mysterious title with a question nobody has asked before.
Write a playful title as a pun or wordplay relating to the topic.
Write a title that hints at a hidden secret or surprising twist.
Write a poetic title using metaphor or imagery drawn from the theme.
structure_variants: Write in a dialogue format, with two voices debating the topic.
Use numbered steps for logical progression, each step gets a quirky subheading.
Compose as a sequence of snippets or diary entries — each dated and distinct.
Scatter random facts or anecdotes throughout, with alternating line breaks.
Build the body as a recipe, with 'ingredients', 'steps', and 'serving suggestions'.
topic_angles: Explore the weirdest product feature you've ever stumbled upon.
Investigate how people use technology in ways its inventors never imagined.
Tell a story about accidentally breaking the rules and what you learned.
Dive into the evolution of internet slang and how language mutates online.
Analyze a viral trend from the perspective of someone baffled by it.
